title: Long Amplicon Nanopore Sequencing for Dual-Typing RdRp and VP1 Genes of Norovirus
author: George Scott

steps.title: All PCR cycling conditions used a...
steps.instruction: All PCR cycling conditions used a heated lid at 105 °C and a ramping speed of 3°C/s. Prepare enough mastermix for all of the samples plus the process and PCR positive and negative controls. GI and GII PCRs are performed independently for each wastewater sample under analysis.

steps.title: This is performed for both the...
steps.instruction: This is performed for both the GI and GII cleaned semi-nested PCR products.

steps.title: At the end of this process,...
steps.instruction: At the end of this process, for every sample being analysed, you will have a combined 200 fmol of GI and GII PCR products comprised of 85.3 and 114.7 fmol of GI and GII amplicons, respectively. The final volume will be 11.5 µL ; ready for use directly in end-prep for library preparation. For samples which do not have sufficient PCR yield (200 fmol of combined GI and GII cleaned, semi-nested PCR products) undiluted, cleaned GI and GII PCR products can pooled and input into end-prep. Adjustment of the quantity of end-prepped DNA input into native barcoding can then be made by varying the sample volume from 0.75-3 µl, reducing the volume of water accordingly, to retain 10 fmol of input DNA.

steps.title: Perform end-prep and barcoding following the...
steps.instruction: Perform end-prep and barcoding following the manufacturer’s instructions for ligation sequencing of amplicons in the Native Barcoding Kit 96 V14 by Oxford Nanopore Technologies

steps.title: Run sequencing at 260 bps using...
steps.instruction: Run sequencing at 260 bps using super-accurate basecalling until ≈60,000 reads per barcode have been generated

steps.title: Install all the required software using mamba
steps.instruction: Install all the required software using mamba: $ mamba create --name=amplion_analysis_norovirus duplex-tools=0.2.14 cutadapt=3.4 seqtk=1.3 minimap2=2.24 yacrd=1.0.0 kma=1.4.9 seqkit=2.3.0 samtools=1.13 bedtools=2.30.0 cd-hit=4.8.1 pyfastx=0.8.4 $ mamba create --name=NGSpeciesID medaka=1.2.2 bcftools=1.11 python=3.6.10 perl=5.32.0 openblas=0.3.3 spoa=4.0.7 racon=1.4.20 minimap2=2.17 tensorflow=2.4.1 $ mamba activate NGSpeciesID $ pip install NGSpeciesID==0.1.3

steps.title: Pipette 25 µL of RNA extract...
steps.instruction: Pipette 25 µL of RNA extract into a 96 well PCR plate

steps.title: Add 45 µL (1
steps.instruction: Add 45 µL (1.8X) of Mag-Bind® Total Pure NGS beads

steps.title: Pipette carefully 10x to mix
steps.instruction: Pipette carefully 10x to mix

steps.title: Leave to stand at room temperature for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Leave to stand at room temperature for 00:05:00

steps.title: If there is liquid on the...
steps.instruction: If there is liquid on the side of the wells, cover the plate with a PCR adhesive seal and briefly spin down plate

steps.title: Place on magnetic rack for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Place on magnetic rack for 00:03:00

steps.title: Remove 68 µL of supernatant
steps.instruction: Remove 68 µL of supernatant

steps.title: Add 80 µL of 80 %...
steps.instruction: Add 80 µL of 80 % (v/v) ethanol for 00:00:30 (make EtOH fresh every time with nuclease free water)

steps.title: Remove 82 µL of supernatant being...
steps.instruction: Remove 82 µL of supernatant being careful to avoid the pellet

steps.title: Repeat clean once more go to step #9
steps.instruction: Repeat clean once more go to step #9

steps.title: Set pipette to 20 µL and remove any residual ethanol
steps.instruction: Set pipette to 20 µL and remove any residual ethanol

steps.title: Leave to dry for 00:02:00
steps.instruction: Leave to dry for 00:02:00

steps.title: Remove from magnetic rack
steps.instruction: Remove from magnetic rack

steps.title: Add 27 µL of molecular grade...
steps.instruction: Add 27 µL of molecular grade water and mix by pipetting 10x

steps.title: Allow to stand for 00:05:00
steps.instruction: Allow to stand for 00:05:00

steps.title: Place plate back on magnetic rack for 00:03:00
steps.instruction: Place plate back on magnetic rack for 00:03:00

steps.title: Recover 25 µL of the supernatant
steps.instruction: Recover 25 µL of the supernatant

steps.title: Add 2
steps.instruction: Add 2.5 µL of LunaScript® RT SuperMix to a 0.2 mL PCR reaction tube placed on ice or on a cool block

steps.title: Add 10 µL of cleaned nucleic...
steps.instruction: Add 10 µL of cleaned nucleic acid extract and mix gently by pipetting 5x

steps.title: Cover or cap the reaction tubes and briefly spin down
steps.instruction: Cover or cap the reaction tubes and briefly spin down

steps.title: Incubate the combined reaction mixture in...
steps.instruction: Incubate the combined reaction mixture in a thermocycler with a heated lid (105°C) for: 25 °C for 00:02:00 55 °C for 00:45:00 95 °C for 00:01:00 Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: Add 7
steps.instruction: Add 7.5 µL of molecular biology grade water to each sample

steps.title: The first-strand cDNA can be stored...
steps.instruction: The first-strand cDNA can be stored in the fridge at 4 °C overnight or in the freezer at -20 °C if longer term storage is required.

steps.title: Briefly spin down the reaction tubes
steps.instruction: Briefly spin down the reaction tubes

steps.title: For best practice, a process control...
steps.instruction: For best practice, a process control negative and positive sample should be implemented from this point onwards. Norovirus GI and GII positive reference materials can be obtained fromthe UK Health Security Agency in the form of Virus LENTICULE‱ Discs. They will need dissolving in 975 µL PBS and then nucleic acid extraction prior to inputting into this process. The inhibitor removal and reverse transcription procedures in this protocol were adapted from the citation below: Harry T Child, Paul A O'Neill, Karen Moore, Hubert Denise, Matthew Loose, Steve Paterson, Ronny van Aerle, Aaron Jeffries. Wastewater Sequencing using the EasySeq™ RC-PCR SARS CoV-2 (Nimagen) V3.0. protocols.io. https://protocols.io/view/wastewater-sequencing-using-the-easyseq-rc-pcr-sar-cih2ub8e

steps.title: Cartridge purified primers should be purchased...
steps.instruction: Cartridge purified primers should be purchased to prevent loss of the degenerateoligonucleotides

steps.title: Reconstitute the lyophilised primers in Table...
steps.instruction: Reconstitute the lyophilised primers in Table 1 to 100 micromolar (µM) using TE buffer pH 8 Table 1. Norovirus GI and GII Primers %s %s

steps.title: Dilute primers to a working concentration...
steps.instruction: Dilute primers to a working concentration of 10 micromolar (µM) in molecular biology grade water and aliquot into suitable volumes to avoid repeated freeze-thaw cycles

steps.title: Prepare the mastermixes as indicated in...
steps.instruction: Prepare the mastermixes as indicated in Table 3 and Table 6 following Table 6. GI and GII primer pairs for the semi-nested PCR %s %s

steps.title: Distribute 20 µL of PCR mastermix into a 96-well plate
steps.instruction: Distribute 20 µL of PCR mastermix into a 96-well plate

steps.title: Vortex and spin down the plate...
steps.instruction: Vortex and spin down the plate from the first round PCR

steps.title: Add 5 µL of first-round PCR...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of first-round PCR product to each of the sample wells

steps.title: Add 5 µL of molecular biology...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of molecular biology grade water for your negative control

steps.title: Seal or cap the plate then...
steps.instruction: Seal or cap the plate then spin down ensuring no bubbles are present

steps.title: Run using the conditions in Table...
steps.instruction: Run using the conditions in Table 7 and Table 8 for GI and GII Table 7. GI semi-nested PCR cycling conditions %s %s Table 8. GII semi-nested PCR cycling conditions %s %s

steps.title: Prepare enough mastermix for all of...
steps.instruction: Prepare enough mastermix for all of the samples and controls from the first-round PCR plus an additional PCR negative control for the semi-nested PCR.

steps.title: Remove ScreenTape from packaging, ensure there...
steps.instruction: Remove ScreenTape from packaging, ensure there are no bubbles in any of the electrophoresis lanes, flicking ScreenTape to remove bubbles if neccesary

steps.title: Load 10 µL of D5000 reagents into the 0
steps.instruction: Load 10 µL of D5000 reagents into the 0.2 mL TapeStation reaction tubes

steps.title: Add 1 µL of ladder into...
steps.instruction: Add 1 µL of ladder into the tube representing position TapeStation position A1

steps.title: Load 1 µL of your positive...
steps.instruction: Load 1 µL of your positive and negative PCR and process controls into the 0.2 mL TapeStation reaction tubes

steps.title: Cap tubes, vortex and then spin...
steps.instruction: Cap tubes, vortex and then spin down ensuring no bubbles are present

steps.title: Place tubes into the machine and analyse the samples
steps.instruction: Place tubes into the machine and analyse the samples

steps.title: All controls should give their expected...
steps.instruction: All controls should give their expected results and appropriate actions should be taken if any of the negative controls show contamination.

steps.title: Prepare the mastermixes as indicated in...
steps.instruction: Prepare the mastermixes as indicated in Table 2 and Table 3 for the first round PCRs Table 2 . Forward and reverse primers for the GI and GII first-round PCRs %s %s Table 3. Mastermix Recipe for all PCRs %s %s

steps.title: Vortex then briefly spin down the prepared mastermix
steps.instruction: Vortex then briefly spin down the prepared mastermix

steps.title: Add 5 µL of molecular biology...
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of molecular biology grade water or positive control cDNA for your PCR positive and negative controls

steps.title: Add 5 µL of cDNA for each sample
steps.instruction: Add 5 µL of cDNA for each sample

steps.title: Distribute 20 µL of PCR mastermix 0
steps.instruction: Distribute 20 µL of PCR mastermix 0.2 mL reaction tubes

steps.title: Seal or cap the reaction tubes...
steps.instruction: Seal or cap the reaction tubes then spin down ensuring no bubbles are present

steps.title: Run the reactions in a thermocycler...
steps.instruction: Run the reactions in a thermocycler using the conditions in Table 4 and Table 5 for GI and GII Table 4. GI first-round PCR cycling conditions %s %s Table 5. GII first-round PCR cycling conditions %s %s

steps.title: Briefly vortex and spin down reaction...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex and spin down reaction tubes or plates from the semi-nested PCR

steps.title: Briefly vortex and spin down ExoSap-IT
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex and spin down ExoSap-IT

steps.title: Aliquot 10 µL of semi-nested PCR...
steps.instruction: Aliquot 10 µL of semi-nested PCR product into a new reaction tube or plate

steps.title: Add 4 µL of ExoSap-IT to each PCR product
steps.instruction: Add 4 µL of ExoSap-IT to each PCR product

steps.title: Seal or cap reaction vessel
steps.instruction: Seal or cap reaction vessel

steps.title: Incubate with a heated lid ( 105 °C )
steps.instruction: Incubate with a heated lid ( 105 °C ): 37 °C for 00:15:00 80 °C for 00:15:00 Hold at 4 °C

steps.title: Remove ExoSAP-IT from the -20 °C...
steps.instruction: Remove ExoSAP-IT from the -20 °C freezer and allow to defrost On ice

steps.title: Add 198 µL High-Sensitivity dsDNA Qubit™...
steps.instruction: Add 198 µL High-Sensitivity dsDNA Qubit™ reagents to Qubit tubes for each sample being quantified

steps.title: Load 2 µL of cleaned, semi-nested...
steps.instruction: Load 2 µL of cleaned, semi-nested PCR product onto the side of the tube ensuring residual nucleic acids aren't present on the exterior of the pipette tip

steps.title: Add 190 µL High-Sensitivity dsDNA Qubit™...
steps.instruction: Add 190 µL High-Sensitivity dsDNA Qubit™ reagents to Qubit tubes for your standards

steps.title: Load 10 µL of each standard
steps.instruction: Load 10 µL of each standard

steps.title: Cap tubes, vortex and briefly spin down
steps.instruction: Cap tubes, vortex and briefly spin down

steps.title: Check for presence of bubbles, flick...
steps.instruction: Check for presence of bubbles, flick tubes and spin down again if necessary

steps.title: Incubate for 00:02:00 and then measure with the Qubit™
steps.instruction: Incubate for 00:02:00 and then measure with the Qubit™

steps.title: Place ScreenTape into the TapeStation and...
steps.instruction: Place ScreenTape into the TapeStation and allow to come to room temperature 00:30:00

steps.title: This is performed for both the...
steps.instruction: This is performed for both the GI and GII semi-nested PCR products.

steps.title: Calculate the volume of water required...
steps.instruction: Calculate the volume of water required to dilute 8 µL of the cleaned semi-nested GI and GII PCR products to 14.83 fmol/µL and 19.94 fmol/µL.

steps.title: Cap the reaction vessels
steps.instruction: Cap the reaction vessels

steps.title: Briefly vortex and then spin down
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex and then spin down

steps.title: In new reaction tubes, for every sample, combine 5
steps.instruction: In new reaction tubes, for every sample, combine 5.75 µL of both the diluted GI and GII PCR products for every sample undergoing analysis

steps.title: Remove all mastermix components from the...
steps.instruction: Remove all mastermix components from the freezer and place the polymerase on On ice

steps.title: Defrost all other components at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Defrost all other components at Room temperature

steps.title: Briefly vortex and spin down all components
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex and spin down all components

steps.title: Add the components as outlined in...
steps.instruction: Add the components as outlined in Table 2 and 3 reserving the polymerase until the end

steps.title: When adding the polymerase, slowly aspirate...
steps.instruction: When adding the polymerase, slowly aspirate and pre-wet pipette tip x3

steps.title: After dispensing the polymerase rinse the pipette tip 10x
steps.instruction: After dispensing the polymerase rinse the pipette tip 10x

steps.title: This step uses a TapeStation for...
steps.instruction: This step uses a TapeStation for PCR product analysis, but gel electrophoresis with 2% agarose tris-borate EDTA gel run at 125 V for 35 min with a 100 bp ladder (Promega, USA), can be used.

steps.title: Remove all mastermix components from the...
steps.instruction: Remove all mastermix components from the freezer and place the polymerase on On ice

steps.title: Defrost all other components at Room temperature
steps.instruction: Defrost all other components at Room temperature

steps.title: Briefly vortex and spin down all components
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex and spin down all components

steps.title: Add the components as outlined in...
steps.instruction: Add the components as outlined in Table 2 and 3 reserving the polymerase until the end

steps.title: When adding the polymerase, slowly aspirate...
steps.instruction: When adding the polymerase, slowly aspirate and pre-wet pipette tip x3

steps.title: After dispensing the polymerase rinse the pipette tip 10x
steps.instruction: After dispensing the polymerase rinse the pipette tip 10x

steps.title: Vortex then briefly spin down the prepared mastermix
steps.instruction: Vortex then briefly spin down the prepared mastermix

steps.title: For each sample, convert the GI...
steps.instruction: For each sample, convert the GI and GII Qubit derived PCR product concentrations from ng/µL to fmol/µL by inputting into software such as the NEBioCalculator ensuring to enter the amplicon lengths of 1110 bp for GI and 971 bp for GII.

steps.title: Calculate the GI dilution factor by...
steps.instruction: Calculate the GI dilution factor by dividing the fmol/µL of the PCR product by 14.83

steps.title: Calculate the volumes of water required...
steps.instruction: Calculate the volumes of water required by subtracting 1 from the dilution factor and then multiplying by 8

steps.title: Calculate the GII dilution factor by...
steps.instruction: Calculate the GII dilution factor by dividing the fmol/µL of the PCR product by 19.94

steps.title: Separately aliquot 8 µL of cleaned,...
steps.instruction: Separately aliquot 8 µL of cleaned, semi-nested GI and GII PCR products into new reaction tubes

steps.title: Add the volumes of water calculated...
steps.instruction: Add the volumes of water calculated in the previous steps to the GI and GII amplicons

steps.title: Vortex and then briefly spin down...
steps.instruction: Vortex and then briefly spin down the cleaned, semi-nested PCR products

steps.title: Briefly vortex and then spin down
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex and then spin down

steps.title: Load 45 fmol of library per...
steps.instruction: Load 45 fmol of library per flow cell, using an amplicon size of 1 kb for molarity calculation

steps.title: Create a folder to store results from the analysis
steps.instruction: Create a folder to store results from the analysis. $ mkdir Analysis_Results $ cd Analysis_Results

steps.title: Copy the fastq_pass or pass folder...
steps.instruction: Copy the fastq_pass or pass folder from the sequencing run to the Analysis_Results folder.

steps.title: Use duplex_tools to split the reads
steps.instruction: Use duplex_tools to split the reads: $ mkdir Split_Reads $ duplex_tools split_on_adapter --threads 12 --allow_multiple_splits \ fastq_pass/${barcodeID} Split_Reads/${barcodeID} Native

steps.title: Store the name of the barcode...
steps.instruction: Store the name of the barcode and sample to be analysed as a variable: $ barcodeID=barcode09 $ sampleID=Sample-4_PCR-Norm_PoolingType-1

steps.title: Create a text file to store...
steps.instruction: Create a text file to store the sequence of different primers being used in the experiment: $ nano primer_sequences.fasta Copy the following into the file and then save it >Long_GI_PCR1 AARYTVCCHATHAARGTTGGNATG...TGTAYAATGGNTGGGTNGG >Long_GI_PCR2 GATGCDGAYTAYACRGCHTGGG...TGTAYAATGGNTGGGTNGG >Long_GII_PCR1 CWGCAGCMCTDGAAATCATGG...ATGTAYAAYGGDYATGCNGGYGG >Long_GII_PCR2 GTGTGRTKGATGTGGGTGACTT...ATGTAYAAYGGDYATGCNGGYGG

steps.title: Use cutadapt to trim primer sequences from reads
steps.instruction: Use cutadapt to trim primer sequences from reads: $ mkdir Trim_Reads $ cat Split_Reads/${barcodeID}/*_split.fastq.gz > Split_Reads/${barcodeID}/combined.fastq.gz $ cutadapt -j12 --action=trim -n 1 -e 0.30 -O 12 --revcomp \ -g file:primer_sequences.fasta --discard-untrimmed \ --output Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-{name}.fastq.gz \ Split_Reads/${barcodeID}/combined.fastq.gz \ > Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}.log 2>&1

steps.title: Select reads which include primers from...
steps.instruction: Select reads which include primers from the 2nd round of PCR and are longer than 800bp, then randomly sample 90,000 reads for subsequent filtering and chimera removal: $ cat Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-*_G*_PCR2.fastq.gz \ > Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.fastq.gz $ seqtk seq -L 800 Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.fastq.gz |\ seqtk sample - 90000 > Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2-gt800bp.fastq

steps.title: Use minimap2 to carry out an all-vs-all alignment of reads
steps.instruction: Use minimap2 to carry out an all-vs-all alignment of reads: $ mkdir Detect_Chimeric_Reads $ minimap2 -k19 -Xw19 -e0 -m100 -r100 -I 30M --cap-kalloc=8000m --cap-sw-mem=100m -t 12 \ Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2-gt800bp.fastq \ Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2-gt800bp.fastq \ > Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap.paf

steps.title: Use yacrd to identify chimeras and reads with poor support
steps.instruction: Use yacrd to identify chimeras and reads with poor support: $ yacrd -t12 -c 10 -n 0.2 \ -i Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap.paf \ -o Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap-gt800bp.report.yacrd $ awk '$1=="NotBad"' \ Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap-gt800bp.report.yacrd |\ cut -f2 \ > Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap-gt800bp.report.lst $ seqtk subseq Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2-gt800bp.fastq \ Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap-gt800bp.report.lst \ > Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2-gt800bp.filtered.fastq $ rm Detect_Chimeric_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.overlap.paf

steps.title: Cluster reads to generate consensus sequences
steps.instruction: Cluster reads to generate consensus sequences: $ mkdir -p Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}/ $ mamba activate NGSpeciesID $ NGSpeciesID --t 20 --q 15 --ont --consensus --max_seqs_for_consensus 100000 \ --abundance_ratio 100 --m 1100 --s 200 \ --fastq Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2-gt800bp.filtered.fastq \ --outfolder Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}/ \ --rc_identity_threshold 0.9 --aligned_threshold 0.8 --mapped_threshold 0.8 \ > Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}/NGSpeciesID.log 2>&1 \ || echo "No Assembly"

steps.title: Align reads against consensus sequences, call...
steps.instruction: Align reads against consensus sequences, call variants and identify any poorly supported regions: $ mamba activate amplion_analysis_norovirus $ cat Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}/consensus_reference_*.fasta \ > Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs.fasta $ kma index -NI -i Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs.fasta \ -o Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs $ mkdir Align_vs_Consensus_Seqs $ kma -i Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.fastq.gz \ -o Align_vs_Consensus_Seqs/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs_kma \ -t_db Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs \ -vcf 1 -ConClave 2 -bcNano -bc 0.7 -bcd 100 -t 20 -md 100 -1t1 \ -ont -ml 900 -xl 1100 -ef -mrs 0.92 -mrc 0.90 \ > Align_vs_Consensus_Seqs/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs_kma.log 2>&1

steps.title: Combine consensus sequences from every sample...
steps.instruction: Combine consensus sequences from every sample into a single file and rename them: $ mkdir Cluster_Consensus_Sequences $ cat Align_vs_Consensus_Seqs/*kma.fsa > Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs.fasta $ seqtk rename Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs.fasta OTU_ \ > Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.fasta

steps.title: Trim consensus sequences to remove any...
steps.instruction: Trim consensus sequences to remove any poorly supported regions identified by kma: $ seqkit locate --bed -P -r -p '^[agct]+' -p '[agct]+$' \ Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.fasta \ > Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.bed $ samtools faidx Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.fasta $ bedtools sort -i Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.bed \ -g Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.fasta.fai \ > Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_sorted.bed $ bedtools complement -i Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_sorted.bed \ -g Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.fasta.fai \ > Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_sorted_inverse.bed $ bedtools getfasta -fi Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed.fasta \ -bed Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_sorted_inverse.bed \ -fo Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed.fasta

steps.title: Align reads against consensus sequences to...
steps.instruction: Align reads against consensus sequences to calculate coverage: $ mkdir Align_vs_Single_Set_Consensus_Reads $ kma index -NI \ -i Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta \ -o Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered $ kma -i Trim_Reads/trimmed-${sampleID}-combined-PCR2.fastq.gz \ -o Align_vs_Single_Set_Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs_kma \ -t_db Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered \ -nc -vcf 2 -ConClave 2 -bcNano -bc 0.7 -bcd 100 -t 20 -md 100 -1t1 \ -ont -ml 900 -xl 1100 -ef -mrs 0.92 -mrc 0.90 \ > Align_vs_Single_Set_Consensus_Reads/${sampleID}_consensus_seqs_kma.log 2>&1

steps.title: Update the sampleID and barcodeID variables,...
steps.instruction: Update the sampleID and barcodeID variables, and repeat steps described in this section for any other samples in the sequencing library.

steps.title: Update the sampleID and barcodeID variables,...
steps.instruction: Update the sampleID and barcodeID variables, and repeat the steps described in this section for any other samples in the sequencing library.

steps.title: Update the sampleID and barcodeID variables,...
steps.instruction: Update the sampleID and barcodeID variables, and repeat the last two steps for any other samples in the sequencing library.

steps.title: Edit the NGSpeciesID script
steps.instruction: Edit the NGSpeciesID script: $ nano ~/mambaforge/envs/NGSpeciesID/bin/NGSpeciesID

steps.title: Change the behaviour of the abundance_cutoff...
steps.instruction: Change the behaviour of the abundance_cutoff parameter so that it is based on the minimum coverage, rather than the relative abundance of a sequence within the sample: - abundance_cutoff = int( args.abundance_ratio * len(read_array)) + abundance_cutoff = args.abundance_ratio

steps.title: Change the behaviour of NGSpeciesID so...
steps.instruction: Change the behaviour of NGSpeciesID so it is possible to output consensus sequences from spoa without polishing the consensus sequences using Medaka: + polishing = False if args.medaka: polishing_pattern = os.path.join(args.outfolder, "medaka_cl_id_*") + polishing = True elif args.racon: polishing_pattern = os.path.join(args.outfolder, "racon_cl_id_*") + polishing = True - for folder in glob.glob(polishing_pattern): - shutil.rmtree(folder) + if (polishing == True): + for folder in glob.glob(polishing_pattern): + shutil.rmtree(folder) spoa_pattern = os.path.join(args.outfolder, "consensus_reference_*") for file in glob.glob(spoa_pattern):

steps.title: Edit NGSpeciesID's consensus module
steps.instruction: Edit NGSpeciesID's consensus module: $ nano ~/mambaforge/envs/NGSpeciesID/lib/python3.6/site-packages/modules/consensus.py

steps.title: Collate a list of all known...
steps.instruction: Collate a list of all known types of norovirus GI and GII from the Centre for Disease Control’s (CDC) Human Calicivirus Typing Tool – this should be performed prior the analysis of any new data sets to prevent missing recently observed types

steps.title: Remove all coverage (coverage_per_genotype_wide
steps.instruction: Remove all coverage (coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv) and sequence data (combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta) relating to consensus sequences that are types not previously reported by the CDC This can be done manually for small data sets or using R and tidyverse 2.0.0.

steps.title: For each wastewater sample under analysis,...
steps.instruction: For each wastewater sample under analysis, annotate the consensus sequences (combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta) with the read depths (coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv) This can be done manually or with R using seqinr 4.2.3 and tidyverse 2.0.0.

steps.title: Perform chimera filtering with USEARCH v11...
steps.instruction: Perform chimera filtering with USEARCH v11 using the following parameters: -uchime3_denovo -chimeras chimeras.fa

steps.title: Use the following files for downstream statistical analysis
steps.instruction: Use the following files for downstream statistical analysis: The coverage of each representative sequence in each sample: Align_vs_Single_Set_Consensus_Reads/*_consensus_seqs_kma.mapstat The sequence of each representative sequence: Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta

steps.title: Open chimeras
steps.instruction: Open chimeras.fa to identify potentially chimeric sequences for that sample

steps.title: Remove all coverage (coverage_per_genotype_wide
steps.instruction: Remove all coverage (coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv) and sequence data (combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta) relating to consensus sequences identified as chimeras in chimeras.fa

steps.title: For each sample, identify types that...
steps.instruction: For each sample, identify types that may be generated from two other (parent) types in the sample using the data in coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv E.g. GI.6[P13] may be a chimera of GI.6[P11] and GI.3[P13] if the latter two have a higher number of reads associated with them.

steps.title: Assess the putative chimeras and parent...
steps.instruction: Assess the putative chimeras and parent sequences using a multiple sequence alignment tool such as NCBI Multiple Sequence Alignment Viewer v1.25.0 with the putative chimera sequence anchored

steps.title: Check the parent sequences for breakpoints...
steps.instruction: Check the parent sequences for breakpoints within the terminal or proximal regions of RdRp and VP1 and child-parent sequence similarities ≥95%

steps.title: Remove all coverage (coverage_per_genotype_wide
steps.instruction: Remove all coverage (coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv) and sequence data (combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta) relating to consensus sequences identified as putative chimeras which match these criteria

steps.title: Using the coverage_per_genotype_wide
steps.instruction: Using the coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv file generated above, for GI and GII independently, sum the norovirus-aligned reads for each sample

steps.title: Find the median of the total...
steps.instruction: Find the median of the total aligned reads per sample across the data set

steps.title: Filter data on a sample-to-sample basis...
steps.instruction: Filter data on a sample-to-sample basis removing reads associated with consensus sequences that have fewer than 0.1% of the median total aligned reads per sample

steps.title: Your data is now ready for downstream analysis
steps.instruction: Your data is now ready for downstream analysis. It should be noted, however, that at present this method is only suitable for qualitative analysis and read-depth/coverage should not be used as a quantitative measurement of the different types detected in a sample.

steps.title: PCR chimeras should be present at relatively low abundances
steps.instruction: PCR chimeras should be present at relatively low abundances. It is worth taking note of the No. of reads aligned with any putative chimeras to avoid the removal of novel recombinants without further investigation.

steps.title: Use the CDC's calicivirus typing tool...
steps.instruction: Use the CDC's calicivirus typing tool to type each representative sequence: https://calicivirustypingtool.cdc.gov/

steps.title: Upload the entire fasta file
steps.instruction: Upload the entire fasta file

steps.title: Click on 'type it'
steps.instruction: Click on 'type it'

steps.title: Once the input sequences have been...
steps.instruction: Once the input sequences have been typed click on 'Download EXCEL'

steps.title: Install R (≥ 4
steps.instruction: Install R (≥ 4.1.2), RStudio (≥ 1.4.1717) and the openxlsx package (≥ 4.2.5.2)

steps.title: Download the coverage files to a...
steps.instruction: Download the coverage files to a sub-folder within the project directory called 'Coverage Results'

steps.title: Setup a new project directory using RStudio
steps.instruction: Setup a new project directory using RStudio

steps.title: Copy the Excel file downloaded from...
steps.instruction: Copy the Excel file downloaded from the CDC's calicivirus typing tool website to the project directory

steps.title: Create a new R script in your RStudio project
steps.instruction: Create a new R script in your RStudio project

steps.title: Copy the following code into the R script
steps.instruction: Copy the following code into the R script: # Load library for opening xlsx files library(openxlsx) # List coverage files coverage_results = list.files(path = 'Coverage Results', pattern = "\\_consensus_seqs_kma.mapstat", recursive = TRUE, include.dirs = FALSE) # List samples samples = gsub(x = coverage_results, pattern = "^(.+)_consensus_seqs_kma.mapstat", replacement = "\\1") # Collate coverage results into a single file combined_coverage_results = NULL for (sampleID in 1:length(samples)) { print(sampleID) coverage_filename = paste0("Coverage Results/", samples[sampleID], "_consensus_seqs_kma.mapstat") coverage_file = readLines(con = coverage_filename) coverage_file[[7]] = gsub(x = coverage_file[[7]], pattern = "# ", replacement = "") noRecords = sum(!grepl(pattern = "##", x = coverage_file,fixed = TRUE)) - 1 if (noRecords > 0) { coverage = read.table(file = textConnection(coverage_file), header = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, sep = "\t", skip = 6) coverage$sample = samples[sampleID] combined_coverage_results = rbind(combined_coverage_results, coverage) } } # Import file linking reference sequences to genotype taxa_results_field_names = c('query','name','sequence','length','Genus','Genotype-B-region-score', 'Genotype-B-region-plot','Genotype','Genotype-C-region-plot', 'Genotype-C-region-score','Genotype-plot') combined_taxa_results = read.xlsx(xlsxFile = "calicivirus_typing_output.xlsx", sheet = "sheet1", startRow = 2) colnames(combined_taxa_results) = taxa_results_field_names # Merge coverage results with genotype combined_coverage_results2 = merge(x = combined_coverage_results, y = combined_taxa_results[,c('name','Genotype')], by.x = c('refSequence'), by.y = c('name'), all.x = TRUE) combined_coverage_results2[ is.na(combined_coverage_results2$Genotype), c('Genotype') ] = "Unknown" # Summarise by genotype coverage_per_genotype = aggregate(formula = readCount ~ Genotype + refSequence + sample, data = combined_coverage_results2, FUN = sum) coverage_per_genotype_wide = reshape(data = coverage_per_genotype, v.names = "readCount", idvar = c("Genotype","refSequence"), timevar = "sample", direction = "wide") coverage_per_genotype_wide[is.na(coverage_per_genotype_wide)] = 0 colnames(coverage_per_genotype_wide) = gsub(x = colnames(coverage_per_genotype_wide), pattern = "^readCount\\.(.+)$", replacement = "\\1") # Save results as a csv file write.csv(x = coverage_per_genotype_wide, file = "coverage_per_genotype_wide.csv", row.names = FALSE)

steps.title: Run the R script, check for...
steps.instruction: Run the R script, check for errors and that the output looks correct.

steps.title: Activate the correct software environment
steps.instruction: Activate the correct software environment: $ mamba activate amplion_analysis_norovirus

steps.title: Cluster consensus sequences at 95 percent sequence identity
steps.instruction: Cluster consensus sequences at 95 percent sequence identity: $ cd-hit-est -i Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed.fasta \ -o Cluster_Consensus_Sequences/combined_seqs_renamed_trimmed_clustered.fasta \ -G 0 -c 0.95 -n 10 -d 0 -M 100000 -T 80 -g 1 -aL 0.9
